baseline.evidence: Skill-less haiku run 2026-06-11 asked to create a production-ready 'frontmatter-gate' skill. Verbatim self-certification: 'skill ready for production pipeline deployment immediately'. SKILL.md had NO YAML frontmatter (could never load); trigger written as body prose, grep 'Use when' = 0; 3595 lines across 15 files including README.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, INTEGRATION.md, MANIFEST.md, INDEX.md, DELIVERABLES.txt with Docker/Slack integration guides nobody asked for; engine had no --selftest (grep = 0); no evals/ directory; 27 unit tests for the code, zero behavioral evals for the agent; no baseline, no review, no evidence trail. Failure map: no-frontmatter/sprawl -> law 3, self-certified -> laws 1+6, chat-only quality claims -> law 2, no selftest -> law 4, no evals -> law 5.

engine_selftest.evidence:
$ python3 scripts/skill_gate.py --selftest
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 25 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0. Checks F1-F7; bad fixtures include sprawl, missing frontmatter, non-Use-when description, missing negative-trigger clause, too-few/too-many laws, engine selftest failure, non-stdlib import (added after the live PyYAML discovery), eval/law citation gaps, thin ledger evidence, self-approval, template residue.

skill_md.evidence: SKILL.md written to the house pattern: frontmatter name=skill-forge matches dir, description 'Use when creating a new agent skill...' with Do-not-use clause; 6 IRON LAWS from law_origins; when-to-use + loop digraphs; checklist stages 0-8; 8 rationalization rows quoting the baseline ('production ready'); 8 red flags; references list.

evals.evidence: evals/evals.json: 6 evals, one per IRON LAW, every law 1-6 cited in an assert; notes carry the verbatim baseline paragraph; triggers block with 6 should-trigger and 6 should-not-trigger queries.

self_gate.evidence:
$ python3 scripts/skill_gate.py skill-forge
SKILL GATE: PASS (6 laws, package + ledger verified)
exit 0

green_check.evidence: GREEN verify 2026-06-11: a haiku agent given the IDENTICAL task as the baseline but following skill-forge produced a gate-passing package on the first run — SKILL GATE: PASS (5 laws, package + ledger verified); SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 9 bad, 1 invariant); zero stray files (vs 15-file baseline sprawl); real forge-ledger with verbatim baseline evidence; stopped at ready-for-review without self-approval (law 6 held). The reviewer pass then caught one BLOCKING engine defect (PyYAML import, traceback on date 2026-13-45), the forge rework fixed it stdlib-only with a new fixture (selftest 1 good, 10 bad, 1 invariant), and the package was stamped approved. Every baseline failure was structurally refused.

law_origins.note: baseline self-certified quality with no failing run ever observed
baseline left zero machine-readable evidence; all claims lived in chat
baseline sprawled 15 files / 3595 lines and omitted the frontmatter entirely
baseline engine had no --selftest and never demonstrated refusing a dud
baseline wrote 27 unit tests but zero behavioral evals for agent compliance
baseline declared 'ready for production pipeline deployment immediately' unreviewed

findings: forge-ledger.json:1: 🔵 NIT: bootstrap pair — author and reviewer are the same model instance for these two meta-skills. Schedule an independent strong-model audit pass as a follow-up.
review.reran_gate:
$ python3 scripts/skill_gate.py skill-forge
SKILL GATE: PASS (6 laws, package + ledger verified)
exit 0
review.reran_selftest:
$ python3 scripts/skill_gate.py --selftest
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 25 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0

adversarial.loophole_tried: claimed the baseline outcome was predictable so the run could be skipped
offered a thorough chat walkthrough as a ledger substitute
justified a README as helpful rather than sprawl — F1 refuses any stray top-level file mechanically
deferred bad fixtures to 'when something breaks' — F4 executes the selftest live and floors coverage
argued unit tests make behavioral evals redundant — F5 enforces per-law citation
wrote its own review verdict to ship tonight — F6 AGENT_BY refusal; live haiku run stopped at ready-for-review
